title: Valid palindrome
description: Determine whether a given string is a valid palindrome after ignoring case and non-alphanumeric characters.
starter: // TODO: normalize the string and compare both ends.
console.log(isPalindrome("A man, a plan, a canal: Panama"));

examples.input: "A man, a plan, a canal: Panama"

constraints: The input is always a string.

title: sum()
description: Create a function sum() that returns the sum of all arguments passed to it.
starter: // TODO: return the total of every argument.
constraints: Do not mutate the arguments list.

title: Count even numbers
description: Determine how many even integers are present in a given array of numbers.
constraints: Input is always a valid array.

title: Two sum
description: Return indexes of two numbers whose sum equals the target.
starter: // TODO: use a map to track seen values.
constraints: You cannot use the same element twice.

title: Largest number formed
description: Arrange non-negative integers to form the largest possible number.
starter: // TODO: sort by combined string order.
constraints: Return the result as a string.

title: Flatten nested array
description: Flatten a deeply nested array without using Array.prototype.flat.
constraints: Support any nesting depth that fits call stack or your iterative approach.

title: Debounce
description: Implement debounce(fn, delay) and preserve arguments and this binding.
starter: // TODO: clear the previous timer and schedule a new call.

examples.input: Call debounced function three times quickly
examples.output: Only the final call runs after delay
fn runs after 300ms of no new calls

constraints: Clear the previous timer before scheduling a new one.

title: Toggle password
description: Create a React component that lets users show and hide a password input.
requirements: Render a password input.
Provide a button to toggle between password and text input types.
Button text should clearly show the current action.
Keep the entered password value when toggling.
edgeCases: Input should not reset when toggled.
The button should be keyboard accessible.
The label should be understandable for screen readers.

examples.output: Input type changes to text and value remains secret

title: Even or odd
description: Create a React component that allows users to input a number and checks whether the number is even or odd. The component displays the result with a short loading delay and handles invalid inputs.
requirements: A user can enter a number in an input field.
A button initiates the check.
Clear any previous result immediately after clicking.
Show "Checking..." for 1 second before displaying the result.
Display "The number X is even." or "The number X is odd."
Show a clear error message for invalid or empty input.
edgeCases: Non-numeric input should display an error.
Empty input should be handled gracefully.
Leading and trailing spaces should be ignored.
Large numbers should be processed correctly.
Result should only appear after loading completes.

title: To-do list
description: Build a to-do list with add, complete, delete, and active count.
requirements: Add a new task from an input field.
Toggle a task between active and completed.
Delete a task.
Show the active task count.
Prevent empty tasks from being added.
edgeCases: Trim spaces before adding.
Keep completed styling distinct.
Use stable ids for list rendering.

title: Accordion
description: Build an accordion where one panel can open at a time.
requirements: Render a list of accordion items.
Open one panel at a time.
Clicking the open panel should collapse it.
Use semantic buttons for headers.
edgeCases: Long content should wrap correctly.
Keyboard users should be able to tab through headers.

examples.output: Section 2 opens and any previous section closes

title: Tabs
description: Create a tab interface with active state and content switching.
requirements: Render tab buttons from data.
Show only the active tab panel.
Highlight the active tab.
Keep the component reusable.
edgeCases: Default to the first tab.
Handle any number of tabs.
Avoid duplicated tab content markup.

title: Stopwatch
description: Build a stopwatch with start, pause, reset, and elapsed time display.
requirements: Start the timer.
Pause the timer.
Reset elapsed time to zero.
Display time in mm:ss format.
Clean up intervals when the component unmounts.
edgeCases: Repeated Start clicks should not create multiple intervals.
Pause should preserve elapsed time.
Reset should stop the timer.

title: Star rating
description: Build an interactive star rating component with hover preview.
requirements: Render five stars.
Hovering previews a rating.
Clicking selects a rating.
Display the selected rating text.
edgeCases: Mouse leave should restore the selected rating.
Initial rating should be zero.
Buttons should be accessible.

examples.output: Four stars look active until mouse leaves
